posts.name: MR ALBERTO VALZOLGHER
posts.cooked: Hi guys, I “recently” moved from Italy to sunny UK. i currently purchase my bottles from Berry Bros. & Rudd, is there any other retailer you can reccomend?

posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked: There’s hundreds! Are you looking at particular regions?

posts.name: MR ALBERTO VALZOLGHER
posts.cooked: i would say my focus would be new world and burgundy/bdx

posts.name: Paul @bbott
posts.cooked:
The Wine Society is my go to for quality and value.

thewinesociety.com

Home

Putting our members before profit, we buy on quality and taste alone so you can enjoy wine at the fairest prices - For you and the people who make it.

link_counts.title: The Wine Society | Free UK Delivery

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: When I lived in the UK, I used to buy mainly from Howard Ripley, Tanners and Loeb. Loeb have obviously had their ups and downs lately, but I was always well-treated and got allocations, as a student, that my spending didn’t really entitle me to.

posts.name: Ian Sutton
posts.cooked:
Agreed on the Wine society - whilst not perfect, and there’s some disquiet about some recent commercial deals, the model is a very good one still, and they’re superb on returns for faulty wine.

Places like Yapp & Howard Ripley are well-established specialists, but especially in London, there are plenty of others. I rather like Uncorked’s range (they’re near ‘the city’).

With wider ranges I do like fareham wine cellar, with some eclectic stuff. Up in Edinburgh, Raeburn wines are absolutely superb, but consistently fail to update their online listing, so a visit recommended. Likewise there’s one in the NW of England whose name escapes me, that’s been a semi-legendary shop for wine pages forumites. If ever wanting larger bottles, localish merchant Imperial Wine (of Bungay) specialise in these.

posts.name: MR ALBERTO VALZOLGHER
posts.cooked: thank you very much everyone for the suggestions, will definitely give them a try… ill also pop into uncorked, turns out is less than 5 min away from my office!

posts.name: Ian Sutton
posts.cooked:
AlbertoV:

ill also pop into uncorked, turns out is less than 5 min away from my office!

I especially like their Portuguese selection, with wines from Soalheiro (vinho verde) and Luis Pato (Bairrada), though their current list doesn’t appear to include Soalheiro.

Kumeu River chardonnays from NZ worth a try

The Italian section seems to be growing, and as you didn’t mention Italy, I presume you have more than enouh of them (but they do a Massa Timorasso)

Plenty of Burgs, Rhone and Bdx, plus a decent Loire selection.

posts.name: Nick Templeton
posts.cooked:
The wine merchant, in the North West, is Byrne & Co, in Clitheroe. It does have a website but its full inventory is only available, if you visit.

Lay & Wheeler is another excellent online merchant, with a really good range of wines, which are usually competitively priced. Lea & Sandeman, with several shops across London, is another decent one. Another vote here for The Wine Society, too.

posts.name: Henry Brown
posts.cooked:
Uncorked is a decent merchant, esp if you work in London/the city.
L&W are great for loose bottles and random things
Stannary are where I’d probably be at for Burgundy
Honest Grapes deserves a mention
Tom Mann Fine Wine probably is some of the best prices going in wine

Other ones worth a mention
Goedhuis
Laithwaites (their IB transfer fees kill me now but they have a decent selection of higher end wines esp Bordeaux during EP)
FRW
Farr Vintners

posts.name: Paul @bbott
posts.cooked: Perhaps David Byrne in Clitheroe, an Aladin’s Cave of a wine shop. Edit, Just seen Nick’s post and confirm.

posts.name: Brian G r a f s t r o m
posts.cooked:
Nick_Templeton:

The wine merchant, in the North West, is Byrne & Co, in Clitheroe. It does have a website but its full inventory is only available, if you visit.

This. So much this. You must go in person. If you have enough time, and are willing to dig, you’ll lay your hands on bottles that likely nobody has seen or touched in years. Super fun store!

And, if you’re all the way up in Clitheroe, bounce on over to Harrogate, and stop in to Harrogate Fine Wines. The shop owner, Andy, is a real gentleman, and is tons of fun to talk with. If you happen to visit, and Andy’s there, tell him I sent ya’ — he’ll get a kick outta that! And, when you’re on your way from one to the other, stop at Wright Wine and Whisky in Skipton — not for the wine, but rather the incredible whisky selection.
posts.display_username: Brian G r a f s t r o m

posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked:
Howard Ripley for burgundy and german Riesling
A&B vintners for burgundy, us pn and chard and various other excellent producers
Tom Mann fine wines for a whole range of stuff
Both Lea & Sandeman and Jeroboams have a good range online and in their shops

There’s at least 20 more I could name!

posts.name: Richard Flack
posts.cooked:
Corney & Barrow for claret used to be VG but it’s ages since I was buying in UK.

Had similar experience with Loeb decades ago as a recent graduate they were most helpful. Unparalleled German selection but that’s not on OP radar. Check Purple Pages for info about the changes there.

posts.name: Daniel Bailey
posts.cooked: Howard Ripley is the one with unparalleled german selection.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: Wish I had known about this when I used to spend time in Alderly Edge, years ago.

reply_to_user.name: Brian G r a f s t r o m

posts.name: Paul @bbott
posts.cooked: If you are in Cheshire, Tanners can be interesting.

posts.name: Brian G r a f s t r o m
posts.cooked: I hear ya’. I didn’t find out about Byrne’s until — ohh — about the last year of our stay. Only went there a couple times, as the last few months saw us trying to deplete our supply to zero (we ultimately got down to two cases…). I remember buying sub-$100 Allemand there.
